help_articles.title: How to reset your forum password
help_articles.body:
This article explains how to reset the password for your support forum account.

1. Go to the forum sign‑in page and click **Forgot your password?** below the login form.
2. Enter the email address associated with your forum account and click **Send reset link**.
3. Check your inbox (and spam/junk folder) for an email titled "Password reset instructions".
4. Click the **Reset password** button in the email. This will open a secure password reset page in your browser.
5. Enter your new password twice. Make sure it is at least 10 characters and includes a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols.
6. Click **Save new password** to confirm.

After resetting, you can sign in with your email and new password immediately. If you do not receive the reset email within 10 minutes:

- Confirm that you typed the correct email address.
- Check spam, junk, and promotions folders.
- Add our noreply address to your safe senders list and request another reset.

If you still cannot reset your password, contact support via the **Contact** link in the footer and include the email address you used to register.

help_articles.title: Reset your password when you no longer have access to your email
help_articles.body:
If you have lost access to the email address registered to your forum account, you may not be able to use the standard **reset password** link. This article explains alternative options.

### Step 1: Try updating your email if you are still logged in
If you are currently logged in on any device:

1. Click your avatar in the top‑right corner and choose **Profile**.
2. Open **Account Settings > Security**.
3. Update your email address to one you can access.
4. Confirm the change via the verification email we send.
5. Use the regular **reset password** process on the sign‑in page if needed.

### Step 2: If you are logged out everywhere
If you cannot sign in anywhere:

1. Go to the **Contact** page from the footer.
2. Select **Account & Login** as the contact reason.
3. Provide the following details:
   - Your current username on the forum
   - The old email address (if you remember it)
   - An estimate of when you created the account
   - Any purchase IDs linked to the account (game editions, DLC, etc.)
4. Our support team will verify ownership and may ask for additional proof.
5. Once verified, we will update your email and send a secure password reset link.

For security reasons, we cannot change an account email or reset a password without sufficient proof of ownership.

help_articles.title: Change or reset your password from the mobile app
help_articles.body:
You can change or reset the password to your gaming support account directly from the official mobile app.

### Change password while logged in

1. Open the app and tap your avatar in the top‑right corner.
2. Tap **Settings**, then **Account & Security**.
3. Choose **Change password**.
4. Enter your current password, then your new password twice.
5. Tap **Save**. Your password will be updated for both the app and the website.

### Reset password if you cannot sign in

1. On the app login screen, tap **Forgot password?**.
2. Enter the email address linked to your account.
3. Check your email inbox on the same device or a computer.
4. Follow the **reset password** link and create a new password.

If you receive an error stating that the email is not found, verify that you created an account with that address and did not sign up through a console‑only profile.

help_articles.title: Two‑factor authentication and password resets
help_articles.body:
Two‑factor authentication (2FA) adds an extra layer of security to your account. This article explains how 2FA affects the **reset password** process.

### When 2FA is enabled

- You can still use the standard **Forgot your password?** link.
- After setting a new password, you will be asked for your 2FA code when signing in.
- If you lose access to your authenticator app, use one of your recovery codes.

### If you have lost both your password and 2FA

1. Go to the **Contact** page and choose **Account & Login**.
2. In the description, explain that you need 2FA removal to reset your password.
3. Include:
   - Username
   - Registered email
   - Recent login regions or platforms
   - Any transaction IDs related to game purchases
4. Our team will review and may ask for additional verification.

We strongly recommend generating new recovery codes and storing them safely after completing a password reset with 2FA enabled.

help_articles.title: Not receiving password reset emails
help_articles.body:
If you requested a password reset but did not receive the email, follow these troubleshooting steps.

1. Wait at least 10 minutes and refresh your inbox.
2. Check your spam, junk, and promotions folders.
3. Search your mailbox for our domain name or for "Password reset instructions".
4. Make sure that you entered the correct email address on the **reset password** page.
5. Add our noreply address to your contacts or safe senders list.
6. Request a new reset email and avoid multiple rapid requests.

Some email providers can delay or block automated messages. If you still do not receive the email after trying all of the above, contact our support team and include your username and the time you attempted the reset.

help_articles.title: Link your console account to the support forum
help_articles.body:
Linking your console account (PlayStation, Xbox, or Switch) to the support forum lets you sync game ownership and access game‑specific areas.

1. Sign in to the forum with your main account.
2. Click **Profile** in the header and go to **Account Settings > Linked Accounts**.
3. Choose your platform: PlayStation, Xbox, or Nintendo.
4. Follow the on‑screen prompts to sign in with your console credentials.
5. Confirm that you grant permission to share basic profile and game ownership data.

Once linked, you will see platform icons next to your username and gain access to platform‑specific support categories.

help_articles.title: Reduce lag and ping spikes in Galaxy Clash
help_articles.body:
If you experience lag spikes or high ping in Galaxy Clash, try the steps below before posting in the Technical Issues forum.

1. Use a wired Ethernet connection instead of Wi‑Fi when possible.
2. Close background downloads or streaming apps on your network.
3. In the game settings, lower your maximum tick rate and disable network smoothing.
4. Choose the server region closest to your location in **Settings > Network**.
5. Power‑cycle your router: unplug it for 30 seconds, then plug it back in.

If lag spikes occur only at certain times of day, your ISP may be congested. Capture a network log or screenshot of your in‑game network graph and include it with any forum post you create about lag issues.

help_articles.title: Galaxy Clash error code 1001 on PC
help_articles.body:
Error code 1001 in Galaxy Clash on PC usually indicates that the game client cannot reach our authentication servers.

### Basic troubleshooting

1. Check the **Server Status** forum to confirm there are no current outages.
2. Temporarily disable VPNs or proxy connections.
3. Make sure your firewall or antivirus is not blocking the game executable or launcher.
4. Restart your PC and router.

### Collecting logs for a bug report

If the error persists, we recommend posting a detailed bug report in **Support > Galaxy Clash > Technical Issues > Bug Report**.

Include the following information in your post:

- Your platform: PC
- Approximate time when error 1001 appears
- Steps to reproduce (for example: launch game, click Play, select region)
- Any error messages shown in the launcher

Providing clear steps makes it easier for our QA and engineering teams to investigate.

help_articles.title: Fix voice chat not working in Battle Royale X on PS5
help_articles.body:
If voice chat is not working in Battle Royale X on PS5, follow these steps before creating a support thread.

1. On your PS5, go to **Settings > Sound > Microphone** and ensure the correct input device is selected and not muted.
2. In Battle Royale X, open **Settings > Audio** and check that **Voice Chat** is enabled and the input/output devices are correct.
3. Test voice chat in another game or in a PS5 party to confirm that your microphone works system‑wide.
4. Check **Privacy > Communication and Multiplayer** on PS5 and confirm that voice communication is allowed.
5. If the issue affects only certain players, make sure that you have not blocked them and that they have not blocked you.

If none of these steps resolves the issue, capture a short video or screenshots of your settings and create a post in the **Battle Royale X > Technical Issues** forum with your PSN ID, region, and time of the last failed attempt.

help_articles.title: Battle Royale X connection issues and NAT types explained
help_articles.body:
Unstable connections, failed lobbies, and frequent disconnects in Battle Royale X are often related to NAT type and router configuration.

### Check your NAT type

1. On console, open your network settings and note your NAT type.
2. Aim for **Open** or **Type 1/Type 2** for the best experience.

### Router configuration tips

- Enable UPnP (Universal Plug and Play) on your router if available.
- Avoid double NAT by using only one router or putting your modem in bridge mode.
- If necessary, port‑forward the ports listed in our official network requirements article.

If you create a thread in **Battle Royale X > Connection Issues**, include your platform, NAT type, and whether you connect via Wi‑Fi or Ethernet.

help_articles.title: Clan & Group Recruitment forum rules
help_articles.body:
The Clan & Group Recruitment forum is designed for players to find groups and clans in a respectful environment.

### Key rules

1. No harassment, hate speech, or personal attacks.
2. Do not spam recruitment posts or bump threads excessively.
3. Clearly label your region and platform in the thread title.
4. Do not share personal contact information such as phone numbers.

If you see harassment or abusive language in a reply, use the **Report** function on that post and select **Harassment/Abuse**, then provide a short explanation. Our moderation team reviews all reports.

help_articles.title: Dragon Quest Arena community guidelines
help_articles.body:
Welcome to the Dragon Quest Arena community! To keep this space friendly and helpful, please follow these rules.

1. Be respectful to other players and staff.
2. No harassment, bullying, or targeted insults.
3. Keep posts relevant to Dragon Quest Arena.
4. Use spoiler tags for story‑related content.
5. Do not advertise unrelated products or services.

By joining the community and posting, you agree to follow these guidelines. You can acknowledge these rules by liking the pinned **Community Rules** thread and posting a short reply stating that you agree.

help_articles.title: How to use the Server Status forums
help_articles.body:
The Server Status forums provide real‑time updates for each region, including EU, NA, and Asia.

### Finding EU server updates

1. Use the search bar in the header and type **server status**.
2. In the filters, select the **EU** tag.
3. Sort results by **Newest** to see the latest updates.
4. Open any recent thread and click **Follow** to receive notifications for future edits and replies.

Server status threads are read‑only for most users; only staff can mark outages as resolved or in progress.

help_articles.title: Requesting a refund for in‑game purchases
help_articles.body:
Refund eligibility for in‑game purchases depends on your platform and local regulations.

1. For console purchases, refunds are usually handled by the platform store (PlayStation Store, Microsoft Store, Nintendo eShop).
2. For purchases made through our PC launcher, you can submit a refund request within 14 days if the content has not been consumed.
3. Visit the **Billing** section of the Help Center for links to platform‑specific refund policies.

When contacting support, include your transaction ID, the item purchased, and the reason for the refund request.

help_articles.title: Update or remove your saved payment methods
help_articles.body:
You can manage your saved payment methods from your account dashboard.

1. Sign in and click **Profile** in the header.
2. Go to **Account Settings > Billing**.
3. Under **Payment Methods**, click **Edit** next to the card or service you want to change.
4. Update the details or click **Remove** to delete the method.
5. Confirm your changes when prompted.

We do not store full card numbers; payment details are handled securely by our payment provider.

help_articles.title: Customize email and on‑site notifications
help_articles.body:
You can control which notifications you receive by email and which appear on the site.

1. Click your avatar in the top‑right corner and select **Settings**.
2. Open the **Notifications** tab.
3. Under **Email notifications**, uncheck any types of emails you do not want to receive.
4. Under **On‑site notifications**, choose which activities (mentions, replies, private messages, etc.) should trigger alerts.
5. In the **Digest** section, set the summary frequency to **Daily**, **Weekly**, or **Monthly**.
6. Click **Save changes** at the bottom of the page.

Adjusting these settings lets you focus on important alerts, such as mentions and replies to your threads, while reducing email clutter.

replies.body:
I had the same lag spikes in Galaxy Clash and managed to fix them with these steps:

1. Switched from Wi‑Fi to a wired Ethernet connection.
2. In **Settings > Network**, I changed my region from AUTO to the closest EU server.
3. Turned off background downloads in both my launcher and OS.
4. In the in‑game **Advanced Network** settings, I set max tick rate to Medium and disabled network smoothing.

After restarting my router and PC, the spikes were completely gone. Worth trying this combination if you haven’t already.
Confirming this helped me as well. The big difference for me was forcing the correct region and turning off my VPN. As soon as I disabled the VPN, ping dropped from ~180ms to 40ms.
If anyone is still seeing spikes after trying the above, run a quick traceroute to the game servers and post a screenshot. That helped support identify a bad ISP route in my case.

Error 1001 went away for me after I:

1. Whitelisted the Galaxy Clash executable in Windows Defender Firewall.
2. Closed my corporate VPN.
3. Logged out of the launcher and logged back in.

If that doesn’t help, grab your latest log files from the launcher logs folder and attach them to your bug report so QA can see what’s happening.

[Staff] We are currently investigating intermittent 1001 errors affecting some PC players in EU. Please include your region, ISP, and approximate time of the error in any new bug reports to help us correlate logs.

A few things to check for Battle Royale X lobby issues:

1. Make sure both you and your friend are on the same server region in **Settings > Gameplay > Region**.
2. If you’re on console, verify that neither of you has cross‑play disabled.
3. Have the party leader restart the game and recreate the lobby.

If that doesn’t work, share your NAT types and platforms so we can dig deeper.

Had the same problem on PS5 + PC cross‑play. Fix was:

- Enabled UPnP on my router.
- Power‑cycled the modem and router.
- After that my NAT type changed from Strict to Moderate and I could join lobbies again.

So definitely check your router config if basic restarts don’t help.

[Support] In addition to the great suggestions above, we’ve seen this resolved by:

1. Clearing the game’s cached data (Options > Storage > Clear Cache on console, or deleting the cache folder on PC).
2. Making sure both players have the same game version and all updates installed.
3. Switching the party leader so the player with the more open NAT hosts the lobby.

If the issue continues after trying these, please post your platform, NAT type, and region so we can investigate individually.
Hey, I’m a casual EU player on PC looking for an active clan. Do you have room for someone who can play a few evenings a week?
We mainly play weekday evenings EU time and focus on co‑op raids. If that works for you, drop your in‑game ID and we’ll send an invite.
Why would we invite you? Your stats are trash and nobody wants a useless player dragging the team down.
Read all the Dragon Quest Arena rules — everything looks fair. Thanks for keeping the community organized.
I agree to follow these community guidelines. Looking forward to joining some arena matches!
[Staff] EU servers are currently experiencing elevated error rates. We are rolling out a fix and expect recovery within 30 minutes. Next update at 04:00 UTC.
[Staff] The EU matchmaking issues reported earlier today have been resolved. Please restart your game client if you still encounter errors.

On PS5, I fixed voice chat in Battle Royale X by:

1. Going to **Settings > Sound > Microphone** and setting Input Device to my headset, then raising the input volume.
2. In‑game, turning **Voice Chat** back ON and setting Voice Channel to Party.
3. Testing my mic in a different game to confirm it wasn’t a hardware issue.

After a full console restart it started working again.

Also make sure your privacy settings allow voice chat: **Settings > Users and Accounts > Privacy > Communication and Multiplayer**. If that’s restricted, the game won’t transmit your voice even if the mic works.

threads.title: Galaxy Clash lag spikes every few seconds (PC)
threads.body:
Hi all,

Since the last Galaxy Clash update Ive been getting really bad lag spikes every 59 seconds on PC. My normal ping is around 40ms but it jumps to 300C500ms briefly and then goes back to normal. It makes ranked unplayable.

Things I already tried:
- Updated GPU and network drivers
- Reinstalled the game
- Restarted my router and PC

Im playing from EU on a wired connection. Is there anything else I can tweak in the inCgame settings or my router to smooth this out?

threads.title: Galaxy Clash error code 1001 on PC after latest patch
threads.body:
Ever since I installed todays Galaxy Clash patch I cant get past the title screen on PC.

I get **error code 1001** right after pressing Play. It was working fine yesterday. Im on Windows 11, EU region, no VPN.

What Ive tried so far:
- Verified game files via the launcher
- Restarted router and PC
- Disabled Windows Defender firewall temporarily

Is this a known issue? Should I post this as a bug report somewhere specific or is there a workaround?

threads.title: Galaxy Clash error code 1001 on PC
threads.body:
Platform: **PC**
Severity: **High**

Im submitting a bug report for repeated error code 1001 in Galaxy Clash on PC.

**Summary**
After the March 3 update, I cannot connect to the game servers. Error code 1001 appears every time I try to enter matchmaking.

**When the error appears**
- Date/time: March 3, 2026, approximately 09:45C10:00 UTC
- Region: EU
- It happens every time I try to queue for any mode.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Launch the Galaxy Clash PC client and log in to my account.
2. From the main menu, select **Play** and choose the EU region.
3. Click **Find Match** for Ranked or Casual.
4. Wait on the matchmaking screen for 5C10 seconds.

**Expected result**
Matchmaking finds a lobby and loads into character select.

**Actual result**
After a few seconds the game shows **"Error code 1001: Unable to reach authentication server"** and returns me to the main menu.

Im not using a VPN or proxy. Ive already whitelisted the game in my firewall and restarted my router and PC. Other online games work fine.

Let me know if you need logs from a specific folder and Ill attach them.

threads.title: Battle Royale X PS5 - voice chat not working in squads
threads.body:
On PS5 my microphone works fine in the console settings and in other games, but in Battle Royale X no one can hear me in squad games.

Details:
- Platform: PS5
- Region: EU
- Voice chat is enabled in the inCgame audio settings
- Headset mic tests fine in PS5 audio settings and in a party chat

Ive restarted the game and the console but it didnt help. Is there anything specific to BRX Im missing? Privacy settings or some inCgame option? No error messages shows up, its just that nobody can hear me.

threads.title: Voice chat not working on PS5 (Battle Royale X)
threads.body:
Hi, Im having trouble with voice chat in Battle Royale X on PS5.

- My headset works in other PS5 games
- Party chat works fine
- In BRX lobbies and matches nobody can hear me, and I dont see the mic icon when I talk

Ive checked that voice chat is enabled in the inCgame audio menu. Is there a known fix for this or some specific PS5 setting I need to change?

threads.title: Can't join friend's lobby
threads.body:
Whenever my friend invites me to a Battle Royale X lobby, I get a "failed to join session" message. If I try to invite them instead, they get the same error.

Details:
- We are both in EU
- CrossCplay is enabled for both of us
- Other online games work fine

Weve tried restarting the game and our routers, but no luck. Is this a known connection issue, or is there a specific port/NAT setting we should be looking at? I wasnt sure which category to post this in, so I put it here for now.

threads.title: [PC][EU] Shadow Legion clan looking for active players
threads.body:
Shadow Legion is recruiting!

- Platform: PC
- Region: EU
- Focus: Competitive PvE and weekend PvP events

We use voice chat for all group content and expect members to be respectful and follow the forum recruitment rules. Post your inCgame ID and a short note about your experience if you want an invite.

threads.title: [EU][All Platforms] EU Raiders recruiting for nightly raids
threads.body:
EU Raiders is a crossCplatform clan running nightly raids and weekly teaching runs.

- Platforms: PC, PS5, Xbox
- Region: EU
- Activity: 19:00C23:00 CET most days

Reply with your platform and time zone if youd like to join. We expect friendly behavior and no harassment toward other players.

threads.title: [NA][Casual] Guardians of Dawn looking for chill players
threads.body:
Guardians of Dawn is a casual clan for players who just want to relax and enjoy the games without strict activity requirements.

- Region: NA

- No minimum power level or playtime

If youre interested, drop a reply with your usual play hours and preferred platform.

threads.title: Dragon Quest Arena Community Rules
threads.body:
Welcome to the Dragon Quest Arena community forum.

To keep this space friendly and useful for everyone, please follow these rules:

1. Be respectful to other players and staff.
2. No harassment, bullying, or targeted insults.
3. Keep posts relevant to Dragon Quest Arena.
4. Use spoiler tags for story and lateCgame content.
5. Do not advertise unrelated products or services.

By participating here, you agree to follow these guidelines. Please like this post and reply below to acknowledge that you have read and agree to the rules.

threads.title: [EU] Server Status - March 3, 2026
threads.body:
[Staff] We are investigating reports of login and matchmaking issues affecting EU servers across multiple titles.

Current impact:
- Intermittent login failures
- Increased matchmaking times

We will post updates in this thread as we have more information. Please follow this topic if you want to receive notifications about changes.

threads.title: [EU] Server Status - March 2, 2026 (Resolved)
threads.body:
[Staff] Earlier today some EU players experienced timeouts when connecting to matchmaking.

The underlying networking issue has been identified and a fix has been deployed. Metrics show that error rates have returned to normal.

If you still encounter matchmaking errors, please restart your game client and try again. If problems persist, create a thread in the appropriate Technical Issues forum with your region and platform.
